The Warriors can then take the skull back to Karaz-a-Karak where the Dwarfs will judge that they have atoned for their wrong doing and reward them with a Treasure card each, taken from the original Warhammer Quest cards.

adventure.name: The Magnificent Four
adventure.flavor: Just for once the Warriors find that their arrival in a village is a cause for celebration rather than disapproval. They are treated to a feast and entertained in high fashion. Realising that something must be up, the Warriors wait for the villagers to make their request. Eventually, towards the end of the evening, the village leaders tell the Warriors that for years they have been harassed by a tribe of Ores, called the Black Fangs, who live in the mountains nearby. The killing and destruction has been bad enough, but during the last raid the Ores found and stole a gold statue that the village treasured. Rumour now has it that the Ores have recently been attacked by Dwarfs and will not be expecting a small raiding party. The villagers believe that it should now be possible for the Warriors to attack the Ore lair and not only get their statue back but put paid to the nasty Ores forever. The leader, holding his hat in his hands, tells the Warriors that they have managed to gather together some gold and pleads for the Warriors to help them.
adventure.rules: To set the Dungeon deck for this adventure, follow the procedure on page 5 of this rulebook. The Warriors will not know where the Shaman's Den or Gorgut's Lair are. When the Warriors leave the first board section the portcullis drops in the doorway. The Warriors cannot get out of the Ores' lair without the key. The key can be found in the Shaman's Den. If the Warriors find Gorgut's Lair before they find the Shaman's Den, place a door at the back of the Lair. The Warriors must continue with the remaining board sections once they have finished in Gorgut's Lair. Use the Gorgut's Lair Objective Room Monster Table to see what Monsters are in the Lair. Once all the Monsters in the Lair have been killed the Warriors can search for the statue, which they will find in the open chest at the back of the Lair. However, just as they lay their hands on the statue, Bogoff the Snotling appears and runs off with it. Place Bogoff at the far end of the board section immediately before Gorgut's Lair. He will run between Gorgut's Lair and the last board section. As with the lantern, the Warriors must hit Bogoff to make him drop the statue. Note which Warrior hits Bogoff. In the Shaman's Den, draw an Event card as normal. Once all the Monsters are dead the key for the portcullis will be found under one of the stools. On the Warriors' return to the village, the villagers will pay them 1D6 x 50 gold each plus an extra 100 gold to the Warrior who got the statue back from Bogoff.

adventure.name: Impossible!
adventure.flavor: The king of Bretonnia claims that his knights have finally rid the Grey Mountains of Ores and Goblins once and for all. His people may now pass safely through the mountains without fear. A banquet is held to celebrate this great day, marred only by the arrival of a deputation from the surrounding villages saying that there seem to be more Ores than ever! The king of course has the villagers whipped soundly and sent away, but just in case there is some truth in the preposterous story he sends the Warriors off to scout around and find out what's going on. In fact, the truth is that a new Warboss called Gorgut had decided to move his tribe, the Black Fangs, from their home near Mount Gunbad. One night he took all his followers and some friendly Night Goblins and went in search of a new lair. After many months of travelling, always by night, they found their new home. Unfortunately Skabnoze, the Shaman who guided them, had a very poor sense of direction. Instead of finding a nice quiet cave complex to the south, say near the Broken Teeth, he headed west, then north and ended up in the Grey Mountains! The Warriors are walking into very dangerous territory, but they have to follow the orders of the king!
adventure.rules: Roll on the Gorgut's Lair Objective Room Monster Table. By the time the Warriors reach Gorgut's Lair they will have realised that there are both Goblins and Ores here. After killing all the Monsters in the lair they will have plenty of proof to take back to the king for their reward, but they cannot leave the lair unless they have found the key to the portcullis. To claim their reward they journey to Couronne to see the king once more. Upon presenting their evidence, the king makes his judgement. Roll a dice and consult the table below. On 1, the proof is ignored and the Warriors are ridiculed as mendacious simpletons, paid nothing, and sent packing. On 2-3, the king and his court refuse to believe that there could be any more Ores and Goblins this far west; however, the Warriors told a jolly tale, and are paid 1D6 x 30 gold each just to go away. On 4-5, the king's advisors see the truth in the Warriors' words and evidence, and the Warriors are paid 1D6 + 6 x 50 gold each as a reward for their heroic quest; knights are once more dispatched to wipe the Ores from the Grey Mountains. On 6, the king deems the Warriors conquering heroes for finding the Monsters and defeating them. They are made honorary knights of the realm, with the right to wear the king's colours. They are also each given an item of treasure from the king's treasure chamber, and each Warrior gets a Treasure card taken from the original Warhammer Quest Treasure deck.

adventure.name: A Hound for a Hound
adventure.flavor: During an Ore raid the favourite hunting hound of Duke Harald of Talabheim was killed when his hunting party was ambushed. Now Harald has become consumed with the idea of revenge and has offered the Warriors a rich reward to enter the lair of the Ore Warboss responsible and kill his favourite Squig Hound; it's only fair, after all. Normally, the Warriors would refuse such a foolhardy and pointless quest, but Harald is an extremely wealthy and powerful duke, and they were supposed to be in charge of the hound when it was killed...
adventure.rules: This adventure is played through like any other, with the following special rules. Each Warrior takes one of the Luck tokens from the Warhammer Quest game, to represent his magic amulet. At any time in the adventure, a Warrior may declare that he is using his amulet. This allows him to re-roll a dice roll which has just failed. Once he has re-rolled, he discards the Luck token, the amulet's power is exhausted, and the Warrior must abide by the second dice roll. When the Warriors reach Gorgut's Lair, use the Gorgut's Lair Objective Monster Table to roll for Monsters as normal but also add Growler the Squig Hound and place him on the platform level. When all the Monsters are reduced to 0 Wounds the Warriors can remove a tooth from the Squig Hound as proof that they killed it and return to Talabheim through a secret passage. Roll on the table below. On 1, the Warriors lose the tooth on the way back and get no reward as the duke does not believe their story. Instead, he kicks them off his lands, never to return. On 2-3, they return with the tooth but the fickle duke has forgotten about the arrangement and gives them each 1D6 x 20 gold to go away. On 4-5, the duke seems happy with the result and pays them each 1D6 x 50 gold as a reward. On 6, the duke declares that he can now sleep happy in the knowledge that he has had his revenge, thanks the Warriors and gratefully pays each of them 1D6 x 100 gold.

adventure.name: Stolen Goods
adventure.flavor: While relaxing in a small tavern between adventures the Warriors are approached by an anonymous Alchemist from Altdorf. The Alchemist wishes to hire the Warriors for a simple job. Rumour has reached him that an evil sorcerer known as Gradluk the Deceiver was recently killed in a wizards' duel in Middenheim. A few years ago Gradluk stole something from the Alchemist and now he wants the Warriors to get it back. Apparently Gradluk had a secret hideaway near Peak Pass, which is where the Alchemist's property is probably hidden. The hideaway will only be lightly guarded and the Warriors should have no trouble in retrieving his property, which will be in a green drawstring bag marked with a skull and crossbones. The mysterious Alchemist tells them the bag contains valuable items that he wants back. However, he says that there are also four healing potions in there, which the Warriors are welcome to if they find the bag. He also gives each Warrior a magical amulet, said to bring luck. The bag is magically trapped to kill anyone who touches it, but the Alchemist provides the Warriors with a counter spell. Only a Wizard can cast the spell. What the Alchemist doesn't know is that soon after the death of Gradluk, a tribe of Ores called the Black Fangs moved into the caves. Retrieving the bag will be by no means easy!
adventure.rules: This adventure is played through like any other, with the following special rules. Each Warrior takes one of the Luck tokens from the Warhammer Quest game, to represent his magic amulet. At any time in the adventure, a Warrior may declare that he is using his amulet. This allows him to re-roll a dice roll which has just failed. Once he has re-rolled, he discards the Luck token, the amulet's power is exhausted, and the Warrior must abide by the second dice roll. In Gorgut's Lair, roll on the Gorgut's Lair Objective Room Monster Table to see what Monsters are in Gorgut's Lair. The Warriors will notice the green bag at the back of the room and will also see a pile of bones and skulls in the corner. Whenever someone upset Gorgut he would make them try and pick up the bag; the skulls are a mute testimony to the power of the protective spell. When all the Monsters have been killed the Wizard can retrieve the bag from the back of the Lair. The Wizard must remember to cast the counter spell before picking up the bag, and he must be standing in the same square as the bag. Roll a D6 and on anything but a score of 1, the Wizard picks up the bag safely; the spell worked. However, if the roll was a 1, black fire consumes him and the flesh is flayed off his bones. His charred skeleton collapses onto the floor. No matter whether the Warriors get the bag or not, the only way out of the lair is back the way they came and there are still likely to be some Monsters around. The Warriors must move back through the rooms and corridors, but there is not an automatic event if they enter a room. If the Wizard rolls a 1 in the Power Phase he must roll again. If the second dice comes up with anything but a 1, take an Event card as normal. However, if the second roll comes up a 1 the Warriors are attacked by Skabnoze and minions. Skabnoze is after the bag now that it is free of its spell and may try to steal it back more than once depending on the Wizard's dice rolls. Once the Warriors get out of the Ores' lair they may go back to see the mysterious Alchemist if they have retrieved the bag. When the Warriors meet up with the Alchemist he checks the bag. Roll a D6: on a roll of 1 Gorgut has thrown out whatever was in the bag and used it to carry his lunch. The bag now contains some well chewed bones and a few lumps of gristle he was saving for a snack. The Alchemist throws the bag down in disgust and refuses to pay the Warriors. On any other roll he is more than happy with the return of his property and gives the Warriors 2D6 x 100 gold each. Note: each healing potion cures 2D6 Wounds.

adventure.name: Generica
adventure.flavor: Usa la ambientacion normal de la Temple of Khaine sin una aventura concreta del Adventure Book.
adventure.rules: Resuelve la sala objetivo con el comportamiento habitual. Si usas esta sala sin una aventura concreta, aplica sus reglas especiales normales y la tabla de monstruos correspondiente.

adventure.name: The Golden Pillars
adventure.flavor: The Warriors encounter Stephano Whitewolf, the leader of a newly organised mercenary company in Couronne known as the Golden Pillars, which consists of a motley group of adventurers. The Golden Pillars are virtuous and upright, trying to subtly direct immoral lords and foil the plans of the wicked. To gain admission into the company and prove their worth, it has been decided that the Warriors must eradicate the growing presence of a Cult of Slaanesh. The faction has recently taken up residence in the sea caves north of Couronne and Stephano wants to remove them before they become a nuisance. The Warriors are given a Golden Pillar, a banner emblazoned with the group's gold emblem, and are told to place it inside the cult's temple in order to complete their membership. Each Warrior should roll 1D6, the one with the highest score being selected to carry the banner. Monsters are at -1 To Hit the Warrior who carries the banner due to its fearsome markings and bold colors. If a Warrior carrying the banner dies, the other Warriors may roll off again to see who gets to pick it up. The whole initiation is merely a formality. Stephano expects the Warriors to simply enter the caverns, run off a few cult members, plant the flag, and return to a hero's welcome. As Stephano figures it, the more members that exist, the more influence his mercenary company will possess, so he always assigns a fairly simple task to newcomers. Unbeknownst to the Warriors and Stephano, the caves have already been cleansed of these cult members by the Hag Queen Melusina and her Dark Elf clan. Her Black Ark raids have been instrumental in maintaining a supply of fresh blood for her magical cauldron, which in turn bestows upon her eternal beauty. Presently, the Black Ark has returned to Naggaroth with slaves and sacrifices, but Melusina and a Dark Elf raiding party remain to terrorise the coast.
adventure.rules: For this Adventure, shuffle the Circle of Power card into the top half of the Dungeon Deck. This Adventure uses the special Hall of the Hag Queen counters. There are books of interest located in the Bottomless Pit, the Water Cave, and the Circle of Power. Each book tells a bit of history, legend, and lore about the Dark Elves, Black Arks, the Cauldron of Blood, and the Hag Queens. For each Book that turns out to be important, you may add +1 to the dice roll when rolling on the Objective Room Monster Table. If there are no Monsters on one of those Board Sections and the party leader is standing on a square that contains a Book, they may pick it up to see if it contains anything important. Take the corresponding Book Counter and the three Skull Counters and put all four of them into a cup. Mix them up and have the party leader pick one from the cup without looking. If it is the Book Counter, put it on the Adventure Record Sheet. If the Warriors draw a Skull Counter instead, the book is unimportant and also trapped, so they must immediately draw an Event Card. Repeat this process for each relevant Board Section. Once the Objective Room is clear of Monsters, a secret passage opens on the wall opposite the entrance. The Warriors plant the Golden Pillar and can leave the room by this exit, which leads up to the surface. Stephano Whitewolf welcomes them into the organisation with an honorary gift from the company vaults, one item of Dungeon Room Treasure to distribute as normal. Bretonnia is also glad to have the Dark Elf presence removed and the King rewards the Warriors with 1D6 x 100 gold each.

adventure.name: Protest
adventure.flavor: The town of St. Leu is refusing to pay taxes because they do not feel their monies are at work protecting them after the third Dark Elf raid in a month, the last of which occurred just before the Warriors arrived. The townsfolk are furious and have begun to steer travellers and merchants away from the area in protest. In a desperate effort to quell the fears of the citizens, the Burgermeister hires the Warriors as mercenaries to pursue the Dark Elf raiders. The Warriors track the Dark Elves to a cavern system on the coast which they bravely enter.
adventure.rules: Take the Torture Chamber Dungeon Room card and shuffle it into the top half of the Dungeon Deck. Shuffle the Temple of Khaine card into the bottom half of the Dungeon Deck for a total of 14 cards. Remove the Execution Event Card from the deck for this Adventure. Instead of drawing an Event Card when the Warriors enter the Torture Chamber, they encounter Executioner Husk Bawn. In addition, draw Event Cards until one reveals Monsters, reshuffling any E Events back into the deck, to see who accompanies him. If the Warriors clear the Board of Monsters there, each Warrior receives one item of Dungeon Room Treasure instead of a single item for the group. If the Warriors find the Temple of Khaine Objective Room and defeat all the Monsters, they discover a false panel in the wall that leads out of the Dungeon and back to the surface. They can return to St. Leu where they are paid 1D6 x 100 gold each by the Burgermeister. In addition, the townsfolk are so relieved that the Dark Elf raids have been stopped that some of the houses donate a family heirloom, an item of Dungeon Room Treasure, to each Warrior.

adventure.name: Hexed
adventure.flavor: Strolling about the streets of Hargendorf in-between adventures, the Warriors inadvertently insult an old wretch begging in the streets. She gives them an evil stare and curses them in a foreign tongue before shuffling off. Not soon afterward bad luck follows the Warriors like an animal stalking its prey. The Warriors have no choice but to seek out the old woman, whom they find in a craggy cave overlooking the sea. As if expecting them, she welcomes them into her hovel, declaring that she will remove the hex if the Warriors bring her the blood of a Dark Elf Hag Queen. Following a pointing finger out toward the ocean, the Warriors can see the vague outline of a dark fortress floating on the water. The Hag Queen awaits deep within the cavernous holds of the foreboding vessel.
adventure.rules: Until the hex is removed, any Warrior who rerolls any dice that successfully alters consequences in their favour must make a Hex Roll immediately afterward by rolling 1D6. This includes Luck Points, Faith Points, the Wizard's Staff, and rerolling dice due to Magic Items, Magic Weapons, Skills, and similar effects. If the result of the Hex Roll is a 1 or 2, the old woman's curse negates the roll as if it never happened. This Hex Roll cannot be rerolled by any means. When the Warriors enter the Objective Room, the Hag Queen Melusina and the old wretch that hexed them in Hargendorf are revealed to be one and the same. Because she was expecting the Warriors, subtract 1 when the Warriors roll on the Dark Elves Objective Room Monster Table. If the Warriors manage to clear the Objective Room, a secret recess opens on the cavern wall opposite the entrance. The small chamber contains captured treasure from various Dark Elf raids as well as a passage to the surface. As the Warriors pass through the doorway, they may each take one random item of Objective Room Treasure before making their way back to shore.

adventure.name: Bloody Retribution
adventure.flavor: It had been days since the Warriors had slept and the weary party spent the night in a grove just a few miles from Bordeleaux. They awoke in chains, being whipped and beaten, and forced to march through a dank underground tunnel by their Dark Elf captors. An encounter with trolls allowed them to break their bonds and escape. They have now made tracks back to Bordeleaux where they plan bloody retribution.
adventure.rules: Take the Dungeon Cell Dungeon Room card and shuffle it in with five other cards to make the top half of the Dungeon Deck. Shuffle the Water Cave Dungeon Room and the Temple of Khaine Objective Room in with five other cards to make the bottom half of the Dungeon Deck. The Warriors begin the Adventure stripped of all of their Weapons, Armour, gold and other items, although they have each managed to locate one hand-to-hand weapon of their choice. They may choose a sword, axe, spear, mace, warhammer, flail or halberd to begin this Adventure. If the Warriors defeat the first Monsters they encounter, they find their lost Weapons. If the Warriors find the Dungeon Cell and clear the Board of Monsters, they find their lost Armour. If the Warriors find the Water Cave and clear the Board of Monsters, they find their lost Gold. If the Warriors find the Temple of Khaine Objective Room and defeat all the Monsters inside, they will find the rest of their missing items. If the Warriors discover the Temple of Khaine before the Water Cave, place the second Doorway on the Temple of Khaine so that the Warriors can continue their search for their gold. Place the three Skull Counters and one of the Book Counters into a cup. Each Warrior receives +1 Attack per turn while their wrath endures. Each time they reveal a new Board Section in the Exploration Phase, each Warrior, starting with the Party Leader, must draw a counter from the cup and return it before the next Warrior draws. If a Warrior draws a Skull Counter, their wrath remains at its peak and they continue to receive +1 Attack per turn. If a Warrior draws a Book Counter instead, their wrath has waned and they no longer receive the bonus. The last Board Section in the Dungeon Deck leads out of the Dungeon and up to the surface. The Warriors have completed their quest and can bask in the satisfaction of revenge.

adventure.name: Rescue Revolt
adventure.flavor: The Bretonnian King's nephew was among the latest victims taken away in a Black Ark raid on the coast of Brionne. The King has demanded that the Warriors rescue his nephew. Although there is no conceivable way to penetrate the magical barrier that surrounds the Black Ark as it sails back to Naggaroth, the King's advisors have devised a desperate scheme: after the royal Enchantress renders the Warriors' weapons invisible by way of a magic spell, the Warriors will allow themselves to be captured by a Dark Elf raiding party. Once inside and after the invisibility spell wears off, they are expected to locate the royal nephew and lead him safely back to Bretonnia. The Warriors see that their best chance of surviving the whole ordeal is to ignite a slave revolt.
adventure.rules: Prepare the Dungeon Deck as normal with one exception: shuffle the Fighting Pit Objective Room into the top seven cards and the Temple of Khaine Objective Room into the bottom four cards to form a total of 13 cards for the Dungeon Deck. Put the Demona Ryme Beastmaster Event Card aside for now. The first Board Section is the Warriors' dungeon cell where the invisibility spell wears off. Draw an Event Card until it reveals Monsters, reshuffling any E Event Cards back into the deck. The Warriors receive the equivalent of an Ambush attack on their astonished captors, taking an extra Warriors' Phase in the first round of combat. If the Warriors defeat their captors they receive one item of Dungeon Room Treasure as normal, and there is only one exit in this room. When the Warriors enter the Fighting Pit Objective Room they are attacked by Beastmaster Ryme, Julong the Wardog, 1 Ogre slave, 1D6 + 3 Hobgoblins slaves, and 1D6 Dark Elves with Crossbows of Strength 4. If Demona Ryme is killed before the Ogre, then the Ogre receives the Break ability. If the Warriors clear the Fighting Pit of Monsters, they can attempt to break down the huge wooden door, Toughness 3 and Wounds 7, and free the captives inside who rush out in droves. The royal nephew is not among them and has been taken to the Temple of Khaine where he will be sacrificed. When the Warriors discover the Temple of Khaine Objective Room in the Exploration Phase, roll 2D6. This is how many turns they have to kill the Hag Queen before she finishes her ritual by plunging a wicked-looking dagger into the royal nephew. Once the Warriors clear the Objective Room, they find a passage out of the Black Ark that leads to the outside world. They leap into the ocean where they are soon rescued by a passing ship. If they have saved the royal nephew, return to the King of Bretonnia and roll 1D6. On 1 to 4, the King is shocked that the Warriors actually completed the mission since it was he who had arranged for the nephew's disappearance in the first place; he grudgingly rewards the Warriors with 1D6 x 100 gold each. On 5 or 6, the King is elated and rewards each Warrior with 1D6 x 100 gold and one item of Objective Room Treasure. If they fail to save the nephew, the Warriors decide a long sabbatical is in order, away from vicious monsters, dangerous dungeons, and irrational rulers with guillotines.

adventure.name: Three Ancient Tomes
adventure.flavor: It is known that the dark caves dotted along the Old World are numerous and, sometimes, enormous. It is also known that their uncharted underground waterways extend from the coast of Estalia to the coast of Kislev, emerging at hundreds of points along the way. The coastal caverns were used decades ago by pirates, smugglers, and traders to evade and circumvent the authorities. When the ocean levels rose, the caverns became all but useless. But the water levels have receded once again and adventurers know that the caves may be ripe with bandit gold and pirate treasure. The Warriors have set their sights on the Old Coast Road near Marienburg, but have come before the High Elves of Marienburg to ask permission, for the High Elves believe that they are the rightful occupants of Marienburg and the surrounding area. They insist that it is written in the Three Ancient Tomes, ancient historical texts that detail the history of their ancestors, the Sea Elves, during the time when they reigned supreme. Unfortunately for them, the texts have been missing for generations. The High Elves, impressed with the Warriors' respect and courtesy, have granted them permission to search for gold and treasure within their ancestral home, but with a single condition: the books would be very valuable to the High Elves, as they would reveal some of the missing history of their kin and may prove the High Elves' claim to the area.
adventure.rules: There are Ancient Tomes located in the Bottomless Pit, Water Cave and Temple of Khaine Board Sections. When there are no Monsters on one of those Board Sections, the Warriors may read a tome to see if it is one they seek. The first time the Warriors find a Tome, put the corresponding Tome Counter into a cup with three Skull Counters and, without looking, have the leader draw one out. If the Tome Counter drawn matches the one on the Board, then it is one of the Three Ancient Tomes the Elves seek. The second time the Warriors find a Tome, put the corresponding Tome Counter into a cup with only two Skull Counters and draw again. When the Warriors come across the third Tome, put that Tome Counter in a cup with only one Skull Counter and draw again. When the Warriors reach the Temple of Khaine Objective Room, roll on the Dark Elves Objective Room Monster Table to see who attacks them. The last card in the Dungeon Deck contains the exit out of the Dungeon. Use the second Doorway in the Temple of Khaine Objective Room to continue through the rest of the Dungeon Deck if appropriate. The Warriors can then return to Marienburg where they deliver any treasure of Elven origin they found. As promised, the High Elves pay twice the gold value of each such treasure. In addition, for every Tome they bring back that discusses Sea Elf history, the Warriors receive one item of Objective Room Treasure, rerolling any treasure of Elven origin, to distribute as normal.
